home.aboutBody1: This website shows four climate indicators for Norway: CO₂ emissions, surface temperature anomaly, projected sea level rise, and historical precipitation. All data comes from open scientific sources.
home.aboutBody2: The numbers are best understood as approximations. Temperature and precipitation come from reanalysis models (NASA GISS and ERA5), which blend observations with atmospheric modelling rather than reading directly from sensors. Sea level projections follow IPCC AR6 global medians and do not account for local land movement or regional ocean variation. CO₂ figures are national totals.
home.aboutBody3: This is a portfolio project, not a scientific tool. For authoritative climate data, consult the Norwegian Environment Agency or the Norwegian Meteorological Institute.

co2.title: CO₂ Emissions
co2.sliderHelp: Drag the slider to explore Norway’s CO₂ emissions year by year. Darker shades mean higher emissions.
co2.sliderAriaLabel: Select year for CO₂ emissions data
co2.chartAriaLabel: Line chart of Norway’s CO₂ emissions over time

temperature.title: Temperature Anomaly
temperature.description: Surface temperature anomaly relative to 1951–1980 baseline
temperature.sliderHelp: Drag the slider to explore Norway’s surface temperature anomaly year by year. Red means warmer than average, blue means cooler.
temperature.sliderAriaLabel: Select year for temperature anomaly data
temperature.chartAriaLabel: Line chart of Norway’s average temperature anomaly over time

sealevel.title: Sea Level Rise
sealevel.description: Projected global sea level rise by emissions scenario (IPCC AR6)
sealevel.sliderHelp:
Drag the slider to explore projected global sea level rise every 10 year (2020-2100), including Norway and coastlines worldwide. The blue overlay shows land areas that would fall below the rising sea level.

Zoom in for more detail along coastlines. Switch between emissions scenarios to compare outcomes.

Projections are global median estimates (IPCC AR6). Local sea level may vary and coastal elevation accuracy is limited, so treat this as an approximation.
sealevel.sliderAriaLabel: Select year for sea level projection
sealevel.chartAriaLabel: Line chart of projected global sea level rise by scenario

precipitation.title: Precipitation
precipitation.description: Historical annual precipitation totals for Norway (ERA5, 1940-2024)
precipitation.sliderHelp:
Drag the slider to explore annual precipitation totals across Norway year by year. Darker blue means more rainfall.

Data covers 1940 to 2024 from the ERA5 reanalysis dataset. Values show average mm per year across grid cells clipped to Norway.
precipitation.chartAriaLabel: Line chart of Norway average annual precipitation over time
